info.title: THE SECURA REST API
info.description:
The API can be used to access:

  • Portfolio

    • Transactions
    • Cash flows
    • Settlement accounts
    • Fee models
    • Positions
    • Performance & benchmark
    • Risk ratios
    • Cost and charges
    • Positions
    • Orders
    • Cashorders
    • Reports
    • Limits

  • Fund accounting and unit ledger

    • Accounts
    • Positions
    • Transactions
    • Orders

  • Static data

    • Securities, indices and currencies
    • Fund data
    • Price data
    • ... and more

Static data in SECURA

All primary keys in SECURA are autoincremented integers, primary keys will be refered to as 'PK' in this documentation and foreign keys will be refered to as 'FK'.
A FK which is nil is represented with the value -1.

User management in SECURA

Use the SECURA Administrator tool to manage API users. An API user can be related to one or several portfolios or a fund account. The user can also be configured as a super user to have access to all portfolios or fund accounts.

Group data

The entities supported in group related endpoints can be seen in the 'entity' parameter under 'Group related data'. Support for more entities will be added over time.

Known issues

Arrays in endpoint parameters does not work in the try it out functions.

get.description: This method can be run in two different ways: 1) To request market values (EoD) for a period, set topPositions = false (default). 2) To request top positions (EoD) for a fund and date, set topPositions = true. In the latter case, you also need to (i) specify a date in the toDate parameter, (ii) specify a fund, and (iii) avoid specifying a customer.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: Format is YYYY-MM-DD. When topPositions = true, fromDate must not specify a date <> to toDate, but it may be left blank. When topPositions = false, fromDate defaults to the first day of the same year as the toDate.

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: Format is YYYY-MM-DD. When topPositions = true, a date must be specified. When topPositions = false, toDate defaults to the current date.

parameters.name: customerid
parameters.description: Defaults to user's customerID. For Fund superuser: selects the customer for which the positions are retrieved. When topPositions is set to true, this parameter must not specify a customer.

parameters.name: fundid
parameters.description: If fundid=0 or not supplied, marketvalues for all customer funds are returned, otherwise the result returned is for this fund only. When topPositions = true, a fund id must be specified.

parameters.name: topPositions
parameters.description: If true, positions for one day and fund, in descending order, are retrieved. In this case, fundid and toDate must, and customerid must not be specifed.

parameters.name: topNPositions
parameters.description: Number of top positions required in the response. If 0, all positions are returned. The parameter is relevant only if topPositions = true.

post.summary: Save new or change current answer.
post.description: This request is for updating or adding KYC answers for one customer or portfolio. It takes a JSON body as input and returns a JSON body with the information that has been saved (if the input data is correctly formatted). You can only provide one of CustomerID/PortfolioID. If the KYCType is 0, you need to put data in the 'AnswerText'-field, if the KYCType is 1 you need to put data in the 'AnswerValue'-field and if the KYCType is 2 or 3, you need to put data in the 'KYCAnswerOptionIds'-field. If you want to change a current answer, you just provied data in the JSON-body as usual with the new answer.

requestBody.description: JSON used for providing the answer.
